subject: Re: Crash when writing to network drive
body:
Hi Evan,

sorry for the late reply – I was hoping you could make use of the better 
error reporting in v1.0.5 to troubleshoot this, but that release is going 
to take one more day to test. Glad everything is working after turning it 
off and on again :-) With tomorrow's release, you will also be able to 
validate the picking accuracy visually thanks to a new micrograph denoising 
algorithm.

Cheers,
Dimitry

On Tuesday, August 7, 2018 at 10:51:20 PM UTC+2, greene4@gene.com wrote:
>
> After restarting the computer it seems to be working just fine. Looking 
> forward to those beautifully picked particles on the fly. 
>
> Cheers,
> Evan
>
> On Monday, August 6, 2018 at 11:15:04 AM UTC-7, gre...@gene.com wrote:
>>
>> Hi Dimitry,
>>
>> Fantastic software! I'm currently having issues being able to write 
>> output files across a network mount. When I run the job locally everything 
>> runs perfectly. However, when attempting to write across our network drive 
>> for on-the-fly processing with Warp the program crashes without any error 
>> message before writing any files. I do not think this is a bug with the 
>> software but as I'm not as familiar with Windows systems communicating with 
>> linux filesystems I was hoping you could advise a possible solution. I'm 
>> currently able to create directories from the Windows machine over the 
>> network from the File Explorer but it seems that Warp cannot. Any 
>> suggestions would be greatly appreciated as we would prefer not to have to 
>> continually transfer data to the local storage. 
>>
>> Thanks,
>> Evan
>>
>
